faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Eltingville home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small holes in wool, silk, or cashmere clothing, webbing in closet corners, small cream-colored larvae on fabrics, and adult moths flying near wardrobes. You may also notice damage to upholstered furniture, rugs, or stored blankets. Our Eltingville technicians provide thorough inspections to confirm infestations and assess the extent of damage.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth control cost in Eltingville?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control in Eltingville typically ranges from $550 to $5,000 depending on the severity of the infestation, size of your home, and number of affected areas. Minor closet treatments start at the lower range, while whole-home infestations requiring multiple treatments and follow-up visits cost more. We provide free estimates and customized treatment plans for your specific situation.

faqs.question: How long does clothes moth treatment take to work?
faqs.answer: Initial treatment immediately begins eliminating adult moths and larvae, but complete eradication typically requires 4-6 weeks with follow-up visits to address newly hatched moths. Our multi-stage approach includes targeted insecticide application, pheromone traps, and heat treatment when necessary. We recommend professional dry cleaning of affected garments and thorough vacuuming to support the treatment process.

faqs.question: Can I prevent clothes moths from returning to my Eltingville home?
faqs.answer: Yes! Prevention includes regular vacuuming of closets and storage areas, storing off-season clothing in sealed containers, using cedar blocks or lavender sachets as natural deterrents, and ensuring proper ventilation in wardrobes. Our technicians provide detailed prevention guidance specific to Eltingville homes and can set up monitoring systems to catch any new activity early before significant damage occurs.
